01 Cover Read before arrival
Prime Movers
Meeting the Moment
May 26 - 28, 2026
Washington, D.C.
02 Welcome — Ambassador A letter from Ambassador Hunt

A message from Swanee.

Dear Prime Movers,

As we stand one week from our gathering in Washington, D.C., I woke up this morning feeling a rush of anticipation of what we'll create together starting Tuesday. I say "starting" because this is no ordinary convening — it is a rare convergence of minds and hearts, where we step into an ever-evolving space designed for the deepest questions of our time.

I'm hosting this gathering because we're at an inflection point in global history, one that demands not just strategy, but soul. Next week we'll have the rare opportunity as a group of some of the most powerful social activists in the world's lone superpower to wrestle with what it means to lead through complexity, division, and possibility.

As you prepare, please consider these guiding questions:

  • Who am I at the core, and why is it right for me to be here, and why am I committed to meeting this historic moment?
  • Who are we at our core — as individuals within this group and as a whole? What strengths can we call on — inside ourselves and collectively — to meet this moment?

These are not questions for exercises, but for the very architecture of our time together. They will frame your/our inquiry, sharpen our dialogue, illuminate the path forward, propel our pace…

It's an honor to host you for what promises to be a transformative encounter — one that will echo in your work and in the world long after we depart.

With eager expectation and love,

Swanee

Ambassador Swanee Hunt
Welcome — Ambassador 02
03 Welcome — Facilitators A letter from the facilitation team

Welcome, Prime Movers.

In a few weeks, two cohorts will meet in DC for several days. The first are Prime Movers — folks who have been doing political, peacebuilding, and movement work in the conditions that make that work hardest. The second are Next Generation Leaders, doing their own version of that work in a political moment none of us trained for. That's the convening we'll be building with you. We're calling it Meeting the Moment, and the title is apt — this is a gathering for the moment we're actually in. The calendar's a coincidence.

We'll be holding the room with you. We're with Centella, where we work with political leaders and movement builders, and convenings like this one (rooms full of people doing real work, in the same physical space, on purpose) are a big part of what we do. Swanee asked us to co-facilitate because the lineage Prime Movers represents is the work we believe in. So: not a neutral facilitation gig for us. We have skin in this game.

In the couple of days we'll spend together, our time will settle into three rhythms. Working sessions on the questions you bring with you. Slower time — time to think, time to figure out what you want to do next, on your own clock. And the rest of it: coffee, walks, and the side conversations that always end up mattering more than what was actually on the agenda.

Come with the questions you don't get to ask anywhere else — the ones you usually shelve because there's nobody around to ask them of. That's what this room is for.

If you hit a snag before then — travel, food, you name it — write us back. We'll fix it, or we'll find who can.

See you soon,

Lisa Changadveja & Pablo Defendini
Co-facilitators · Centella
03 Welcome — Facilitators
04 Thought piece A written contribution from each Prime Mover
A thought piece, from you.

Each Prime Mover is invited to contribute a written thought piece for the cohort to read in advance. Topic is your choice — what you're thinking about, what you're working on, what you're stuck on, what you'd argue with the room about.

Length: 700–1,500 words. Long enough to make an argument; short enough that everyone will actually read it.

Form: prose, not bullet points. First person. Drafts welcome — polish is not the bar.

These will be circulated before the convening and used as inputs to Day 1 mapping. The best work in this room has always started with someone putting a position on paper.

Submit to
yo@centellaglobal.com
Due by
Friday, 8 May 2026
Format
Word doc, Google Doc link, or plain-text email
Come ready to share.

We're making time across the convening for musical and cultural performances — songs, readings, anything you'd like to bring. If you play an instrument, bring it along.

Next Generation 08

Pre-Packet · single-page format

Same visual system as the program brochure — sunny cream paper, WWP navy ink, punchy orange + amber accents, Fira Sans throughout, Cormorant italic as second voice, no eyebrows. Recast to single-page 6×9 sheets (10 total) instead of facing 12×9 spreads.

Format
6×9 portrait single-page. Ten sheets. Print-ready at the same trim as the heritage option, or distributed as a PDF before the convening.
Page count
1 Cover · 2 Welcome (Ambassador) · 3 Welcome (Facilitators) · 4 Agenda · 5 Thought-piece request · 6 Venues · 7 Prime Movers (plus special guest) · 8 Next Generation
Content state
All copy is placeholder. The structure, visual hierarchy, and tone-coded panels are the deliverable; words come from Val (Ambassador), the facilitation team, Hunt Alternatives (cohort list), and Jason (map + directions).
Accent discipline
Every bright fill takes navy text on top. Body copy clears WCAG AAA on cream throughout (12.8:1+).
